rationale: Codebase-pin lens: ran the full plan §Bead 2 Verification checklist fresh against a2007086, tree clean. All 7 items pass. V1: TestPanel_GateFieldDecisionInert PASS (also fresh -count=1). V2: internal/panel package green, exit 0. V3: `go list -deps ./internal/panel | grep internal/config` returns non-zero — leaf invariant holds (AC11 first half). V4: `threshold > 0` guard present at gate.go:257 (AC11 second half). V5: ADR carries 'spec 112' + 'decision-inert'; amendment count HEAD=8 > parent a7b432ad=7; ADR diff is purely additive (zero removed content lines) so 109's §3 note is byte-untouched. V6: architecture.md doc-sync in HEAD commit. V7: `go build ./...` exit 0; `go vet ./internal/panel` clean. Structural inertness confirmed: no non-test code reads Panel.Gate (only a Bead-3 doc comment at config.go:820); gate.go and ApproveThreshold never reference it. Decoupling verified — internal/panel depends on neither internal/instruct (z4ps) nor internal/harness.
findings: Every Bead-2 verification command reproduced green independently. Decision-inertness is not just asserted but structurally guaranteed: a repo-wide `.Gate` word-boundary search finds zero non-test readers of the new field. The ADR amendment is correctly homed under §1 (schema block) alongside the abandon_reason/099/102/106 precedents, purely additive, §§3/6/8 unchanged. Nit (non-blocking): the checklist's amendment-count grep is coarse (counts the substring 'amendment' anywhere), but manual inspection confirms exactly one new §1 note and 109's §3 note intact, so the check's intent is satisfied.
